sources.label: ANDRITZ secures hydropower capacity expansion contracts with COPEL (Energy Global, Jun 2, 2026 - predates window, booked in Q1 intake)
ANDRITZ receives major upgrade order for three NZ hydropower plants (company newsroom, Apr 2026 - predates window)

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6; the COPEL (Jun 2) and Mercury NZ (Apr) hydro orders predate the window and were already in Q1 order intake. Yesterday's down move already absorbed the adjusted-rank recalibration, and today's -0.65% drift gives no reason to move again on this strict latent-node screen.
verdicts.bull: Record EUR 3.6B Q1 order intake and a swelling hydro backlog (COPEL +2.1 GW, Mercury NZ +76 MW) make Andritz a quiet beneficiary of the power-constrained grid buildout that AI demand is amplifying, with hydro/pumped-storage cycles running to 2030.
verdicts.neutral: Solid hydro/process franchise at $8.65B cap with +14.3% YTD, but the AI linkage is second-derivative (grid power and water systems, not compute), so it earns its low strict-screen rank; nothing this week changes the evidence grade.
verdicts.bear: The AI-chain pathway is the most diluted on the list - pulp & paper and metals cyclicality dominate the P&L, and if European capex softens the hydro order momentum won't shield earnings; the YTD run already prices in the backlog story.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: H1 2026 results / continued hydro mega-orders (pumped storage tenders in Europe, Brazil, India) confirming multi-year backlog growth tied to grid expansion.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Cyclical slowdown in pulp & paper and metals divisions masking hydro strength, plus the thesis remaining too far from the AI compute bottleneck to ever rerate on the theme.
